vocab.title: Vocabulary
vocab.description: Build your French vocabulary with essential words and phrases.
tips: Focus on pronunciation: listen to each word carefully.
Pay attention to gender (masculine/feminine) for nouns.
Try to use new words in sentences to remember them better.
Review weak words regularly to solidify learning.
Example: un livre (a book), une voiture (a car), j'achète un livre.

connectors.title: Connectors (Connecteurs)
connectors.description: Master French discourse connectors to link ideas naturally in conversation and writing.
tips: Connectors help you organize thoughts: cause, consequence, opposition, addition.
Each connector has specific uses—pay attention to when to use each one.
Practice using connectors in sentences to improve fluency.
These are essential for advanced writing and formal speech.
Examples: Je suis fatigue, donc je dors. Il pleut, mais je sors.
More examples: Je travaille, donc je progresse. Il est tard, pourtant je continue.

pronoms relatifs.title: Relative Pronouns (Pronoms Relatifs)
pronoms relatifs.description: Three-group method: learn Group 1 first, then build Groups 2 and 3 directly from it.
tips: Group 1 (after a preposition other than de): lequel (m.s.), laquelle (f.s.), lesquels (m.pl.), lesquelles (f.pl.).
Examples Group 1: La chaise sur laquelle je suis assise... Les raisons pour lesquelles nous sommes parties...
Group 2 (with a prepositional phrase in de, like pres de, a cote de, au centre de): add de to Group 1 forms.
Forms Group 2: duquel, de laquelle, desquels, desquelles.
Examples Group 2: Le bateau a bord duquel j'ai traverse le fleuve. Le parc au centre duquel il y a de vieux arbres.
Group 3 (when the relative verb is built with a): add a to Group 1 forms with contractions.
Forms Group 3: auquel, a laquelle, auxquels, auxquelles.
Contractions to remember: a + lequel = auquel, a + lesquels = auxquels, a + lesquelles = auxquelles.
Examples Group 3: L'entreprise a laquelle nous avons consacre tant d'efforts... Les evenements auxquels vous faites reference...

subjonctif & indicatif.title: Subjunctive vs. Indicative (Subjonctif & Indicatif)
subjonctif & indicatif.description: Quick rule: use subjonctif for uncertainty/emotion (WEIRD), and indicatif for facts or what you believe to be true.
tips: Subjonctif = WEIRD: W = Want (je veux que), E = Emotion (je suis content que), I = Impersonal expression (il faut que), R = Recommendation (il est important que), D = Doubt (je doute que).
Formation (subjonctif present, most verbs): take the present 'ils' form, remove '-ent', then add endings: -e, -es, -e, -ions, -iez, -ent.
Remember: nous and vous endings in subjonctif (-ions, -iez) look like imparfait endings.
Subjonctif examples: Je veux qu'il vienne. Il faut que nous partions. Je doute qu'ils aient raison.
Indicatif = facts, certainty, or what you treat as true.
Indicatif examples: Je pense qu'il vient. Je crois qu'elle est prete. Je sais qu'ils arrivent.
More indicative examples: Je crois qu'il comprend. Je vois qu'elle travaille.

émotions et sentiments.title: Emotions & Feelings (Émotions et Sentiments)
émotions et sentiments.description: Express your emotions and feelings naturally in French using authentic expressions.
tips: Use 'ça me rend' + adjective to describe how something makes you feel.
Use 'ça m'émeut' for emotional impact or movement.
Use 'ça m'inquiète', 'ça me tracasse' for worry and concern.
Use 'ça m'irrite', 'ça m'énerve' for annoyance.
These expressions are much more natural than simple adjectives alone.
Examples: Ca me rend triste. Ca m'inquiete. Ca m'enerve.
More examples: Cette chanson m'emeut. Son retard me tracasse.

mixed.title: Mixed Categories
mixed.description: Practice all categories together to reinforce your French learning across different domains.
tips: Mixed mode combines vocabulary, connectors, relative pronouns, subjunctive/indicative, emotions, and conjugation.
This helps you develop well-rounded French skills.
Pay attention to which category each question belongs to.
Use this mode to review and consolidate your learning.
Example review set: un livre, donc, qui, il faut que..., ca m'inquiete.
Mixed sentence example: Je veux qu'il vienne, car ca m'inquiete quand il est en retard.

conjugation.title: French Verb Conjugation (Conjugaison)
conjugation.description: Timeline view: move from past to present to future, and use each tense based on when the action happens.
tips: Read left to right: distant past -> recent past -> now -> near future -> distant future.
Each tense card shows exactly how to form it and one model sentence.

timeline.exampleFrench: Quand il est arrive, j'avais deja mange.
timeline.exampleEnglish: When he arrived, I had already eaten.

timeline.exampleFrench: Quand j'etais petit, je jouais ici.
timeline.exampleEnglish: When I was little, I used to play here.

timeline.formation: Present of etre + en train de + infinitive
timeline.exampleFrench: Je suis en train de lire.

timeline.formation: Use present forms for tu/nous/vous (subject omitted)

timeline.exampleFrench: Je vais manger dans une heure.
timeline.exampleEnglish: I am going to eat in an hour.
Tomorrow, I will speak with him.

timeline.formation: Futur simple of avoir/etre + participe passe
timeline.exampleEnglish: I will have finished before your return.
